# High-volume host/action pairs from a delimited field with negative indexing

**Dataset**: `gx_08_feed`

Threshold-style detection over a pipe-delimited `combo` field (`user|host|action|count`): keep only events whose count is high, then roll up the loudest host+action combinations. It `split`s the field, reads `host` and `action` by positional `arrayindex`, and crucially reads the trailing count with **negative index** `arrayindex(parts, -1)` so it works regardless of how many leading fields exist. A pre-aggregate `filter cnt >= 3` prunes low-volume noise, then `comp ... by host, action` aggregates and a HAVING-style `filter hits >= 1` runs on the result. Gotcha embodied: `arrayindex(parts, -1)` grabs the last element (robust to schema drift), and the two `filter`s sit on opposite sides of `comp` — one on raw rows, one on computed aliases.

```sql
dataset = gx_08_feed
| alter parts = split(combo, "|")
| alter host = arrayindex(parts, 1), action = arrayindex(parts, 2), cnt = to_number(arrayindex(parts, -1))
| filter cnt >= 3
| comp count() as hits, sum(cnt) as vol by host, action
| filter hits >= 1
| sort desc vol, asc host
```
